figureTitle: The self-sealing prediction loop vs. the provisional alternative
figureType: cycle diagram with adjacent linear chain (systems/causal diagram)
desc: Routing tasks by predicted proficiency creates a circular, self-confirming loop, whereas a high-expectation teacher's provisional belief runs a linear, evidence-updating chain that never seals.
subtitle: Self-confirming loop versus open evidence-updating chain

figureTitle: Two parallel calibration problems
figureType: two-column comparison panel with shared base row (conceptual map)
desc: Problem-solving teaching and teacher expectations are the same structural problem — calibration — each with a mechanism, an AI default that opposes it, and a resulting conflict.
subtitle: Calibration = withholding certainty while evidence arrives

data.axisLabel: Calibration = withholding certainty while evidence arrives

figureTitle: Two influences against the Hattie hinge
figureType: horizontal bar chart with reference line
desc: The group's two influences — problem-solving teaching (d = 0.68) and teacher expectations (d = 0.43) — both sit above the d = 0.40 hinge, but with a meaningful gap between them.
subtitle: Both clear the hinge, with a gap between them

figureTitle: The configuration fork: guardrailed vs. unguardrailed
figureType: four-curve line chart (two-panel divergence/convergence comparison)
desc: Same model, students, and material split by one configuration setting: guardrailed practice and exam curves converge (+127% practice, ~0 exam) while unguardrailed diverge (+48% practice, -17% exam).
subtitle: Practice rising, exam falling — the Bastani signature

figureTitle: The pilot loop: diagnosis, teaching, verification
figureType: three-panel horizontal flow with return loop (systems diagram)
desc: The chemistry pilot's value came from a three-stage loop — platform diagnoses, humans teach, platform verifies — feeding human decisions rather than replacing them.
subtitle: The platform feeds human decisions, not replaces them

figureTitle: Tutoring effect sizes vs. hinge and human-tutor benchmark
figureType: horizontal dot/bar plot with two reference lines
desc: ITS systems average d = 0.48 but reach d = 0.76 under step-level conditions — near the human-tutor benchmark of d = 0.79 — while the group's three rows all clear the 0.40 hinge.
subtitle: Step-level ITS (0.76) nears the human-tutor mark (0.79)

figureTitle: Mechanism deletion: embodied vs. AI-mediated delivery
figureType: grouped horizontal bar chart with reference line
desc: Through embodied co-presence, cohesion (0.44) and adventure (0.43) clear the hinge as large effects; delivered through AI both collapse to a hypothesized d ~ 0.05 — a near-total deletion, not a discount.
subtitle: Embodied effects collapse to near zero through AI

figureTitle: Cohesion as risk-tolerance infrastructure
figureType: two-state side-by-side comparison panel (structural schematic)
desc: In a low-cohesion classroom, participation is a gamble that exits through a social-cost door; in a high-cohesion classroom the gamble collapses and high-value behaviors become structurally available.
subtitle: Low trust sets the cognitive-output floor

rows.left: Output floor set by lowest safety tolerance

figureTitle: Nora's assembled evidence file
figureType: four-quadrant component layout (conceptual map)
desc: AI assembled four components for the program's defense: a participation-outcome correlation table, an equity-gap map, alumni theme clusters, and an administrative-hour audit converting recovered hours into expanded sessions.
subtitle: Four AI-assembled components build the defense

children.label: Alumni theme clusters (30 years of survey text)
Admin-hour audit: recovered hours to expanded sessions

figureTitle: Bandura's four efficacy sources, by strength
figureType: vertical ranked stack with adjacent capability columns (systems diagram)
desc: Collective efficacy grows from four sources ranked by strength — mastery experience (strongest, self-compounding), vicarious experience, social persuasion (weak alone), affective state — and the dashboard touches only source 3.
subtitle: The dashboard touches only social persuasion

children.label: Social persuasion (weak alone; dashboard touches this)

figureTitle: The largest hypothesized collapse: 1.57 to 0.15
figureType: two-bar comparison chart with hinge reference line
desc: Collective teacher efficacy carries the dataset's largest effect size (d = 1.57) as a converted correlation, but hypothesized survival under AI-dashboard-only delivery is d ~ 0.15 — the largest hypothesized collapse in the book.
subtitle: Measured 1.57 vs. AI-dashboard-only survival 0.15

figureTitle: Belief is the fuel, not the engine
figureType: horizontal causal fan-in (systems diagram)
desc: Collective belief changes outcomes only by running through four behavioral channels — attempt more, persist longer, attribute struggle to instruction, cooperate at higher bandwidth — every one terminating in teacher behavior.
subtitle: Four channels converge on teacher behavior

figureTitle: Teacher-model complementarity over the school year
figureType: multi-line trend chart (prediction-accuracy over time)
desc: Teacher judgment starts high and plateaus while the model starts low and rises steeply; the combined ranking runs above both after the first grading period — teachers win at the information-poor start, models catch up late.
subtitle: Teachers win early; models catch up; combined wins late

data.axisLabel: Prediction accuracy across the school year

markers.label: Year-end
markers.sub: Model highest of the single signals

figureTitle: The two-student audit: where model and teacher disagree
figureType: two-case side-by-side comparison panel (structural schematic)
desc: Two week-nine cases show why model output and teacher knowledge must stay distinct — a quiet high performer flagged red by an absence artifact, and a struggling student shown green by mere completion. The disagreement is the signal.
subtitle: The disagreement is the most valuable signal

rows.left: Teacher: knows the flag is wrong

figureTitle: Two influences against the hinge
figureType: horizontal bar chart with hinge reference line
desc: Teacher estimates of achievement (d = 1.29) and teacher clarity (d = 0.75) both sit in the High band, far above the 0.40 hinge, with estimates near the top of the entire list.
subtitle: Estimates near the top of the whole list

figureTitle: The algorithmic Matthew effect loop and its exit
figureType: cycle diagram with dotted exit branch (systems diagram)
desc: An adaptive platform initialized on prior score serves easier content, generates less growth, and reads that as confirmation — a self-sealing loop that opens only through a structured exit (human override plus scheduled re-estimation).
subtitle: Self-sealing loop with a structured exit

figureTitle: Same data, two uses: diagnosis vs. measurement
figureType: two-column contrast fork from a shared source (conceptual map)
desc: Identical assessment data becomes either a short-shelf-life instructional plan (act, re-diagnose, revise) or a long-shelf-life placement label (route, schedule, confirm), depending entirely on what the institution does with it.
subtitle: Shelf life decides whether data hardens into destiny

data.axisLabel: Same assessment data, two institutional uses

figureTitle: Same number, opposite classification
figureType: horizontal bar chart with classification color encoding and hinge line
desc: Prior ability (d = 0.94, context/destiny-risk) sits one rank above strategy to integrate with prior knowledge (d = 0.93, teachable lever) — near-identical numbers, opposite classifications — while prior achievement (d = 0.55) shows how construct definition moves the number.
subtitle: 0.94 and 0.93: nearly identical, opposite classifications

items.label: Strategy to integrate with prior knowledge (lever)

figureTitle: Three properties a chatbot cannot have
figureType: three-panel structural comparison (comparison panels)
desc: Live seminar is public, plural, and shared-context-building — properties a private student-AI dialogue lacks by construction. The d = 0.82 was earned by the live version only.
subtitle: The d = 0.82 was earned by the live seminar only

data.axisLabel: Evidence base: d = 0.82 belongs to the live form

figureTitle: AI at the edges of the talk, never its center
figureType: weekly timeline with role-placement arrows (cycle/flow diagram)
desc: Across a seminar week AI appears at three points — Monday rehearsal, Thursday analytics, weekend prep — all pointing toward the single live seminar on Wednesday, never replacing it.
subtitle: Every AI stage serves the Wednesday seminar

stages.label: Monday: AI rehearsal (student generates, AI cross-examines)
Thursday: AI analytics (talk map to instructor)

figureTitle: The asymmetry vendors omit
figureType: two-bar comparison with hinge reference line
desc: The Murphy et al. meta-analysis behind the d = 0.82 shows strong, reliable comprehension gains but rare, unreliable critical-thinking gains — an asymmetry the aggregate number hides.
subtitle: Comprehension reliable; critical thinking rare

figureTitle: The two-line graph: assisted vs. unassisted
figureType: two-line trend chart with policy-change inflection (time series)
desc: Assisted (hint-available) accuracy climbs from week one while unassisted (cold-set) accuracy stays flat until the configuration changes at week eight — attempt-first, fading active, cold-set mastery — after which the unassisted line finally rises.
subtitle: The cold-set line is the only one that matters

markers.label: Week 1
markers.sub: Assisted rises; unassisted flat and low

figureTitle: The scaffolding contract: two phases and the failure mode
figureType: horizontal two-phase progression with a divergent failure branch (mechanism diagram)
desc: Legitimate scaffolding runs two phases — supported then faded — while the commercial failure mode keeps phase one running indefinitely and declares mastery from assisted answers ('dependence with good UX').
subtitle: Fading is the load-bearing condition

stages.label: Phase 1: supported (high contingency, help at the edge)
Failure mode: Phase 1 forever, mastery declared from assisted answers

data.note: The failure branch keeps Phase 1 running and never fades.

figureTitle: The Macnamara correction: variance explained
figureType: horizontal bar chart (percentage of variance explained)
desc: Deliberate practice explains about 26% of performance variance in games, 21% in music, 18% in sports — and only about 4% in education — capping the practice-quantity story exactly where practice platforms are sold.
subtitle: Education's 4% caps the practice-quantity story

figureTitle: Two students, two preparation paths
figureType: two-path comparison panel (process comparison)
desc: Student A reads an AI summary five times (recognition only) while Student B writes from memory, checks against the chapter, and fixes gaps — engaging retrieval, generation, monitoring, and error correction. B is more prepared though A feels more prepared.
subtitle: B is more prepared though A feels more prepared

figureTitle: Three acts on the production side
figureType: production-side three-act schematic with deletion contrast (mechanism diagram)
desc: Producing a summary forces three cognitive acts at once — selection, generation, self-testing — all of which a received summary deletes by completing them elsewhere.
subtitle: A received summary deletes all three acts

data.axisLabel: Three cognitive acts: run vs. deleted

figureTitle: Automaticity: working-memory reallocation
figureType: before/after capacity-partition comparison (mechanism cross-section)
desc: Under heavy decoding load a child's working memory is mostly consumed by decoding, leaving little for comprehension; after repeated reading with error feedback automatizes decoding, capacity is released and comprehension expands.
subtitle: Repeated reading releases capacity for comprehension

rows.right: Decoding shrinks to a small region

figureTitle: The substitution ledger: five roles, three verdicts
figureType: five-row classification roster (conceptual map / structural schematic)
desc: Repeated reading breaks into five roles with different substitutability verdicts — reader (never), text (AI-generatable with QC), listener/error-catcher (AI with caveats), prosody model (human-preferred), motivation/relationship (human) — a roster rather than a gate.
subtitle: A roster of roles, not a single gate

figureTitle: The listening economy, before and after
figureType: before/after allocation comparison (proportional schematic)
desc: Before AI, the teacher's scarce listening time is spread thin across all 26 children; after, AI listens to the 18 stronger readers while the teacher concentrates on the 8 lowest — expanding the listening economy rather than replacing its human share.
subtitle: AI expands listening; the teacher concentrates on the 8 lowest

data.axisLabel: Allocation of listening time across 26 children

rows.right: AI listens to the 18 stronger readers
